# rom.rom_utils { #skrom.rom.rom_utils }

`rom.rom_utils`

Utilities for ROM workflows.

The module includes functions for:
- snapshot index splitting for training and testing
- parameter sampling (Sobol, Latin hypercube, Gaussian)
- basis updates by deflation and augmentation
- reconstruction from reduced to full coordinates
- ROM data save/load helpers
- Newton solvers for reduced and hyper-reduced systems
- residual collection routines used in hyperreduction

## Notes {.doc-section .doc-section-notes}

Authors: Suparno Bhattacharyya; Ali Hamza Abidi Syed

## Functions

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [collect_residuals](#skrom.rom.rom_utils.collect_residuals) | Evaluate and store residual samples used in hyperreduction training. |
| [collect_residuals_ecm](#skrom.rom.rom_utils.collect_residuals_ecm) | Evaluate and store ECM residual samples at the element–Gauss-point level. |
| [collect_residuals_t](#skrom.rom.rom_utils.collect_residuals_t) | Evaluate residual samples for a set of snapshot groups. |
| [compute_nonlinear_snapshots](#skrom.rom.rom_utils.compute_nonlinear_snapshots) | Evaluate a function on each (solution, parameter) pair and stack results. |
| [generate_gaussian_samples](#skrom.rom.rom_utils.generate_gaussian_samples) | Generate samples from normal draws based on bounds. |
| [generate_lhs](#skrom.rom.rom_utils.generate_lhs) | Generate Latin hypercube samples and scale them to bounds. |
| [generate_sobol](#skrom.rom.rom_utils.generate_sobol) | Generate Sobol samples and scale them to bounds. |
| [latin_hypercube_train_test_split](#skrom.rom.rom_utils.latin_hypercube_train_test_split) | Create train/test masks by ordering indices from a Latin hypercube draw. |
| [load_rom_data](#skrom.rom.rom_utils.load_rom_data) | Load ROM data from a ROM_data folder. |
| [newton_hyper_rom_solver](#skrom.rom.rom_utils.newton_hyper_rom_solver) | Newton solve for a reduced system defined by assemble_func. |
| [newton_hyper_rom_solver2](#skrom.rom.rom_utils.newton_hyper_rom_solver2) | Hyper-ROM Newton wrapper: |
| [newton_solver_rom](#skrom.rom.rom_utils.newton_solver_rom) | Single-mode ROM Newton: always solve (A * delta = -y) each iteration |
| [reconstruct_solution](#skrom.rom.rom_utils.reconstruct_solution) | Map reduced coordinates to full coordinates and add the mean. |
| [rom_data_gen](#skrom.rom.rom_utils.rom_data_gen) | Save ROM outputs to a ROM_data folder. |
| [select_elements_and_gauss_weights](#skrom.rom.rom_utils.select_elements_and_gauss_weights) | Build a mapping from element index to a weight list per Gauss point. |
| [sobol_train_test_split](#skrom.rom.rom_utils.sobol_train_test_split) | Create train/test masks by ordering indices from a Sobol draw. |
| [solve_linear](#skrom.rom.rom_utils.solve_linear) | Solve A x = b with a chosen backend. |
| [train_test_split](#skrom.rom.rom_utils.train_test_split) | Create boolean masks that split snapshot indices into train and test sets. |
| [update_basis](#skrom.rom.rom_utils.update_basis) | Update a basis by deflating snapshots and appending modes from an SVD. |

### collect_residuals { #skrom.rom.rom_utils.collect_residuals }

```python
rom.rom_utils.collect_residuals(
    NLS_train_ms,
    NLS_train_mean,
    V_sel,
    reconstruct_solution,
    Residual,
    training_params,
    assemble_kwargs,
    extra_kwargs=None,
    hyper_basis=None,
)
```

Evaluate and store residual samples used in hyperreduction training.

For each snapshot:
- project snapshot to reduced coordinates
- reconstruct a field
- build keyword arguments by assemble_kwargs
- call Residual.hyperreduction(**kw)
- stack results along axis 0

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name                 | Type           | Description                                                                | Default    |
|----------------------|----------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| NLS_train_ms         | ndarray        | Mean-shifted snapshots.                                                    | _required_ |
| NLS_train_mean       | ndarray        | Mean field.                                                                | _required_ |
| V_sel                | ndarray        | Basis used for projection and reconstruction.                              | _required_ |
| reconstruct_solution | callable       | Function that maps reduced coordinates to full coordinates.                | _required_ |
| Residual             | object         | Object with method hyperreduction(**kw) that returns an array.             | _required_ |
| training_params      | sequence       | Parameter list aligned with NLS_train_ms.                                  | _required_ |
| assemble_kwargs      | callable       | Function that maps (u_recon, param) to a dict for Residual.hyperreduction. | _required_ |
| extra_kwargs         | dict or None   | Dict merged into kw.                                                       | `None`     |
| hyper_basis          | object or None | If given, call hyper_basis.interpolate(u_recon) before assemble_kwargs.    | `None`     |

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type    | Description               |
|--------|---------|---------------------------|
| q_mus  | ndarray | Stacked residual samples. |

### collect_residuals_ecm { #skrom.rom.rom_utils.collect_residuals_ecm }

```python
rom.rom_utils.collect_residuals_ecm(
    NLS_train_ms,
    NLS_train_mean,
    V_sel,
    reconstruct_solution,
    Residual,
    training_params,
    assemble_kwargs,
    extra_kwargs=None,
    hyper_basis=None,
)
```

Evaluate and store ECM residual samples at the element–Gauss-point level.

For each snapshot this routine:
- projects the mean-shifted snapshot to reduced coordinates,
- reconstructs the full field,
- evaluates ``Residual.hyperreduction_ecm(**kw)``, and
- stacks the transposed result so each snapshot contributes a block of shape
  ``(r, n_elements * n_q)``.

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name      | Type                                                | Description                           |
|-----------|-----------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------|
| q_mus_ecm | (ndarray, shape(n_snapshots * r, n_elements * n_q)) | Snapshot matrix used by ECM training. |

### collect_residuals_t { #skrom.rom.rom_utils.collect_residuals_t }

```python
rom.rom_utils.collect_residuals_t(
    NLS_train_ms,
    NLS_train_mean,
    V_sel,
    reconstruct_solution,
    Residual,
    training_params,
    assemble_kwargs,
    snapshot_downsampling=1,
    extra_kwargs=None,
    hyper_basis=None,
)
```

Evaluate residual samples for a set of snapshot groups.

This variant loops over an outer list/array of snapshot groups and
applies downsampling by index.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name                  | Type           | Description                                                                     | Default    |
|-----------------------|----------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| NLS_train_ms          | sequence       | Collection of snapshot groups.                                                  | _required_ |
| NLS_train_mean        | sequence       | Collection of mean fields aligned with snapshot groups.                         | _required_ |
| V_sel                 | ndarray        | Basis used for projection and reconstruction.                                   | _required_ |
| reconstruct_solution  | callable       | Function that maps reduced coordinates to full coordinates.                     | _required_ |
| Residual              | object         | Object with method hyperreduction(**kw) that returns an array.                  | _required_ |
| training_params       | sequence       | Parameter list. Kept for interface consistency.                                 | _required_ |
| assemble_kwargs       | callable       | Function that maps a reconstructed field to a dict for Residual.hyperreduction. | _required_ |
| snapshot_downsampling | int            | Keep one snapshot per this interval.                                            | `1`        |
| extra_kwargs          | dict or None   | Dict merged into kw.                                                            | `None`     |
| hyper_basis           | object or None | If given, call hyper_basis.interpolate(u_recon) before assemble_kwargs.         | `None`     |

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type    | Description               |
|--------|---------|---------------------------|
| q_mus  | ndarray | Stacked residual samples. |

### compute_nonlinear_snapshots { #skrom.rom.rom_utils.compute_nonlinear_snapshots }

```python
rom.rom_utils.compute_nonlinear_snapshots(
    non_linear_func,
    fos_solutions,
    param_list,
)
```

Evaluate a function on each (solution, parameter) pair and stack results.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name            | Type     | Description                                                           | Default    |
|-----------------|----------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| non_linear_func | callable | Function with signature non_linear_func(u=<solution>, param=<param>). | _required_ |
| fos_solutions   | sequence | Sequence of full-order solutions.                                     | _required_ |
| param_list      | sequence | Sequence of parameters aligned with fos_solutions.                    | _required_ |

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type    | Description                         |
|--------|---------|-------------------------------------|
| F_nl   | ndarray | Stacked outputs of non_linear_func. |

### generate_gaussian_samples { #skrom.rom.rom_utils.generate_gaussian_samples }

```python
rom.rom_utils.generate_gaussian_samples(dimensions, num_points, bounds)
```

Generate samples from normal draws based on bounds.

Mean is (lower+upper)/2.
Std is (upper-lower)/5.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name       | Type                          | Description                        | Default    |
|------------|-------------------------------|------------------------------------|------------|
| dimensions | int                           | Count of parameters.               | _required_ |
| num_points | int                           | Count of samples.                  | _required_ |
| bounds     | list\[tuple\[float, float\]\] | (lower, upper) for each parameter. | _required_ |

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name    | Type                                     | Description                                        |
|---------|------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------|
| samples | (ndarray, shape(num_points, dimensions)) | Samples from normal draws. No clipping is applied. |

### generate_lhs { #skrom.rom.rom_utils.generate_lhs }

```python
rom.rom_utils.generate_lhs(dimensions, num_points, bounds)
```

Generate Latin hypercube samples and scale them to bounds.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name       | Type                          | Description                        | Default    |
|------------|-------------------------------|------------------------------------|------------|
| dimensions | int                           | Count of parameters.               | _required_ |
| num_points | int                           | Count of samples.                  | _required_ |
| bounds     | list\[tuple\[float, float\]\] | (lower, upper) for each parameter. | _required_ |

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name           | Type                                     | Description                 |
|----------------|------------------------------------------|-----------------------------|
| scaled_samples | (ndarray, shape(num_points, dimensions)) | Samples in parameter space. |

### generate_sobol { #skrom.rom.rom_utils.generate_sobol }

```python
rom.rom_utils.generate_sobol(dimensions, num_points, bounds)
```

Generate Sobol samples and scale them to bounds.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name       | Type                          | Description                                                        | Default    |
|------------|-------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| dimensions | int                           | Count of parameters.                                               | _required_ |
| num_points | int                           | Count of samples. Input is used as 2**m with m = log2(num_points). | _required_ |
| bounds     | list\[tuple\[float, float\]\] | (lower, upper) for each parameter.                                 | _required_ |

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name           | Type                                     | Description                 |
|----------------|------------------------------------------|-----------------------------|
| scaled_samples | (ndarray, shape(num_points, dimensions)) | Samples in parameter space. |

### latin_hypercube_train_test_split { #skrom.rom.rom_utils.latin_hypercube_train_test_split }

```python
rom.rom_utils.latin_hypercube_train_test_split(N_snap, train_percentage=0.8)
```

Create train/test masks by ordering indices from a Latin hypercube draw.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name             | Type   | Description                         | Default    |
|------------------|--------|-------------------------------------|------------|
| N_snap           | int    | Count of snapshots.                 | _required_ |
| train_percentage | float  | Fraction assigned to the train set. | `0.8`      |

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name       | Type                             | Description             |
|------------|----------------------------------|-------------------------|
| train_mask | (ndarray\[bool\], shape(N_snap)) | Mask for train indices. |
| test_mask  | (ndarray\[bool\], shape(N_snap)) | Mask for test indices.  |

### load_rom_data { #skrom.rom.rom_utils.load_rom_data }

```python
rom.rom_utils.load_rom_data(self, rom_data_dir=None)
```

Load ROM data from a ROM_data folder.

#### Behavior {.doc-section .doc-section-behavior}

- If self is None, return (fos_solutions, sim_data_dict).
- If self is not None, set attributes on self and return None.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name         | Type                | Description                                                       | Default    |
|--------------|---------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| self         | object or None      | Target instance that holds problem_name, or None for return mode. | _required_ |
| rom_data_dir | str \| Path \| None | Path to ROM_data, or a module path string, or None for auto path. | `None`     |

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type                              | Description                      |
|--------|-----------------------------------|----------------------------------|
|        | (fos_solutions, sim_data) : tuple | Returned only when self is None. |

### newton_hyper_rom_solver { #skrom.rom.rom_utils.newton_hyper_rom_solver }

```python
rom.rom_utils.newton_hyper_rom_solver(
    assemble_func,
    u,
    tol=0.03,
    maxit=200,
    param=None,
)
```

Newton solve for a reduced system defined by assemble_func.

assemble_func(u, param) must return (A, y) such that:
    A * delta = -y

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name          | Type     | Description                                               | Default    |
|---------------|----------|-----------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| assemble_func | callable | Function with signature (A, y) = assemble_func(u, param). | _required_ |
| u             | ndarray  | Initial iterate. Updated in place.                        | _required_ |
| tol           | float    | Stop threshold on \|\|delta\|\|.                          | `0.03`     |
| maxit         | int      | Iteration cap.                                            | `200`      |
| param         | any      | Input passed to assemble_func.                            | `None`     |

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type    | Description    |
|--------|---------|----------------|
| u      | ndarray | Final iterate. |

### newton_hyper_rom_solver2 { #skrom.rom.rom_utils.newton_hyper_rom_solver2 }

```python
rom.rom_utils.newton_hyper_rom_solver2(
    J_rom_fn,
    rhs_rom_fn,
    u0,
    *J_args,
    tol=0.01,
    maxit=50,
    alpha=1.0,
    damp_freq=40,
    rhs_args=(),
    linear_backend='numpy',
    linear_method='cg',
    linear_rtol=1e-08,
    linear_atol=0.0,
    linear_maxit=500,
    petsc_pc='ilu',
    petsc_options=None,
    verbose=True,
)
```

Hyper-ROM Newton wrapper:
  J_rom_fn(u, *J_args) -> J(u)   (dense reduced Jacobian)
  rhs_rom_fn(u, *rhs_args) -> R(u) (reduced residual)
Solves: J(u) * delta = -R(u)

### newton_solver_rom { #skrom.rom.rom_utils.newton_solver_rom }

```python
rom.rom_utils.newton_solver_rom(
    assemble_func,
    u0,
    *args,
    tol=0.01,
    maxit=100,
    alpha=1.0,
    damp_freq=40,
    linear_backend='numpy',
    linear_method='cg',
    linear_rtol=1e-08,
    linear_atol=0.0,
    linear_maxit=500,
    petsc_pc='jacobi',
    petsc_options=None,
    verbose=True,
    **kwargs,
)
```

Single-mode ROM Newton: always solve (A * delta = -y) each iteration
using the selected linear backend (dense direct or iterative).

### reconstruct_solution { #skrom.rom.rom_utils.reconstruct_solution }

```python
rom.rom_utils.reconstruct_solution(u_reduced, V_sel, mean)
```

Map reduced coordinates to full coordinates and add the mean.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name      | Type                     | Description                                                          | Default    |
|-----------|--------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| u_reduced | ndarray                  | Reduced coordinates. Shape can be (r,) or (n, r) depending on usage. | _required_ |
| V_sel     | (ndarray, shape(N_h, r)) | Basis matrix used for reconstruction.                                | _required_ |
| mean      | (ndarray, shape(N_h))    | Mean field used in centering.                                        | _required_ |

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name       | Type    | Description                          |
|------------|---------|--------------------------------------|
| u_complete | ndarray | Reconstructed field with mean added. |

### rom_data_gen { #skrom.rom.rom_utils.rom_data_gen }

```python
rom.rom_utils.rom_data_gen(save_kw, problem_path)
```

Save ROM outputs to a ROM_data folder.

This function stores:
- fos_solutions.npy
- ROM_simulation_data.npz (all other items from save_kw)

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name         | Type        | Description                                     | Default    |
|--------------|-------------|-------------------------------------------------|------------|
| save_kw      | dict        | Data to save. Must contain key "fos_solutions". | _required_ |
| problem_path | str or Path | Path to the problem folder.                     | _required_ |

#### Raises {.doc-section .doc-section-raises}

| Name   | Type     | Description                                   |
|--------|----------|-----------------------------------------------|
|        | KeyError | If "fos_solutions" is not present in save_kw. |

### select_elements_and_gauss_weights { #skrom.rom.rom_utils.select_elements_and_gauss_weights }

```python
rom.rom_utils.select_elements_and_gauss_weights(
    n_gauss_points,
    element_indices,
    weights,
)
```

Build a mapping from element index to a weight list per Gauss point.

Input indices are Gauss-point indices. The mapping includes one entry per
element that appears in element_indices. For each mapped element, the list
length equals n_gauss_points. Unselected Gauss points keep weight 0.0.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name            | Type              | Description                                      | Default    |
|-----------------|-------------------|--------------------------------------------------|------------|
| n_gauss_points  | int               | Gauss point count per element.                   | _required_ |
| element_indices | sequence\[int\]   | Gauss-point indices selected by a sampling step. | _required_ |
| weights         | sequence\[float\] | Weights aligned with element_indices.            | _required_ |

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name                     | Type                       | Description                                                   |
|--------------------------|----------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------|
| element_to_gauss_weights | dict\[int, list\[float\]\] | Map element_id -> list of weights with length n_gauss_points. |

### sobol_train_test_split { #skrom.rom.rom_utils.sobol_train_test_split }

```python
rom.rom_utils.sobol_train_test_split(N_snap, train_percentage=0.8)
```

Create train/test masks by ordering indices from a Sobol draw.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name             | Type   | Description                         | Default    |
|------------------|--------|-------------------------------------|------------|
| N_snap           | int    | Count of snapshots.                 | _required_ |
| train_percentage | float  | Fraction assigned to the train set. | `0.8`      |

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name       | Type                             | Description             |
|------------|----------------------------------|-------------------------|
| train_mask | (ndarray\[bool\], shape(N_snap)) | Mask for train indices. |
| test_mask  | (ndarray\[bool\], shape(N_snap)) | Mask for test indices.  |

### solve_linear { #skrom.rom.rom_utils.solve_linear }

```python
rom.rom_utils.solve_linear(
    A,
    b,
    *,
    backend='numpy',
    method='cg',
    rtol=1e-08,
    atol=0.0,
    maxit=500,
    petsc_pc='jacobi',
    petsc_options=None,
)
```

Solve A x = b with a chosen backend.

Notes:
  - CG requires SPD.
  - MINRES requires symmetric (can be indefinite).
  - GMRES works for general matrices.

### train_test_split { #skrom.rom.rom_utils.train_test_split }

```python
rom.rom_utils.train_test_split(N_snap, N_sel=None, train_percentage=0.8)
```

Create boolean masks that split snapshot indices into train and test sets.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name             | Type        | Description                                                            | Default    |
|------------------|-------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| N_snap           | int         | Count of snapshots.                                                    | _required_ |
| N_sel            | int or None | Count of indices to select before the split. If None, use all indices. | `None`     |
| train_percentage | float       | Fraction assigned to the train set.                                    | `0.8`      |

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name       | Type                             | Description             |
|------------|----------------------------------|-------------------------|
| train_mask | (ndarray\[bool\], shape(N_snap)) | Mask for train indices. |
| test_mask  | (ndarray\[bool\], shape(N_snap)) | Mask for test indices.  |

### update_basis { #skrom.rom.rom_utils.update_basis }

```python
rom.rom_utils.update_basis(V, W_mu, max_modes=5)
```

Update a basis by deflating snapshots and appending modes from an SVD.

#### Steps {.doc-section .doc-section-steps}

1) Deflate snapshots: W_deflated = W_mu - V(V^T W_mu)
2) Compute SVD of W_deflated
3) Append up to max_modes left singular vectors
4) Re-orthonormalize by QR

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name      | Type                         | Description                              | Default    |
|-----------|------------------------------|------------------------------------------|------------|
| V         | (ndarray, shape(N_h, r_old)) | Basis matrix.                            | _required_ |
| W_mu      | (ndarray, shape(N_h, N_t))   | Snapshot matrix for one parameter value. | _required_ |
| max_modes | int                          | Cap on appended modes.                   | `5`        |

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type                             | Description           |
|--------|----------------------------------|-----------------------|
| V_new  | (ndarray, shape(N_h, r_old + k)) | Updated basis matrix. |
